title: A Man Young And Old:- Human Dignity
content:
Like the moon her kindness is,
If kindness I may call
What has no comprehension in�t,
But is the same for all
As though my sorrow were a scene
Upon a painted wall.

So like a bit of stone I lie
Under a broken tree.
I could recover if I shrieked
My heart�s agony
To passing bird, but I am dumb
From human dignity.
meaning:
"A Man Young And Old:- Human Dignity" is a quintessential example of William Butler Yeats's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
